channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Remembering Clarence B. Jones, MLK adviser who helped write 'I Have a Dream' speech
item.description: Clarence B. Jones, a lawyer and adviser to the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr., died last week. Jones helped King write his famous "I Have a Dream" speech. He was 95.
item.content:encoded: Clarence B. Jones, a lawyer and adviser to the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr., died last week. Jones helped King write his famous "I Have a Dream" speech. He was 95.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: Boston-area Haitians waiting for price drop as World Cup game nears
item.description: The Haitian community in Massachusetts is excited about the country participating in a World Cup for the first time since 1974. But pricey tickets and restrictive immigration policies are deterrents.
item.content:encoded: The Haitian community in Massachusetts is excited about the country participating in a World Cup for the first time since 1974. But pricey tickets and restrictive immigration policies are deterrents.
item.dc:creator: Simon Rios

item.title: As Trump embraces a gas tax holiday, there's a much bigger problem
item.description: President Trump's calls for a gas tax holiday raise a bigger issue: The gas tax is no longer covering the cost of the nation's highways anyway, and the problem will only get worse.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's calls for a gas tax holiday raise a bigger issue: The gas tax is no longer covering the cost of the nation's highways anyway, and the problem will only get worse.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Knicks superfan Spike Lee says his team will definitely win the Championship
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with filmmaker Spike Lee about his excitement that his favorite basketball team, the New York Knicks, making it to the NBA finals.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with filmmaker Spike Lee about his excitement that his favorite basketball team, the New York Knicks, making it to the NBA finals.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: Black homeowners in St. Louis are still awaiting aid a year after destructive tornado
item.description: Decades of disinvestment in a predominantly Black St. Louis neighborhood left the community especially vulnerable to last year's devastating tornadoes. Now, some worry homeownership rates will drop.
item.content:encoded: Decades of disinvestment in a predominantly Black St. Louis neighborhood left the community especially vulnerable to last year's devastating tornadoes. Now, some worry homeownership rates will drop.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Henderson

item.title: FBI trains local and state police on how to stop growing threat of drones
item.description: The upcoming FIFA World Cup is prompting the FBI to ramp up training for law enforcement on how to stop a growing threat: malicious drones in U.S. skies.
item.content:encoded: The upcoming FIFA World Cup is prompting the FBI to ramp up training for law enforcement on how to stop a growing threat: malicious drones in U.S. skies.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: A Union fort in Tennessee will honor the history of Black laborers who built it
item.description: The history of a union fort in Tennessee is getting an update to include the story of the Black laborers who built it.
item.content:encoded: The history of a union fort in Tennessee is getting an update to include the story of the Black laborers who built it.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Abrams

item.title: The U.S. government wants to treat any Americans with Ebola outside of the U.S.
item.description: The U.S. plans to try to keep Ebola out by keeping citizens who were potentially exposed in Kenya. This has some in Kenya frustrated and others worried it will deter aid workers from helping.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. plans to try to keep Ebola out by keeping citizens who were potentially exposed in Kenya. This has some in Kenya frustrated and others worried it will deter aid workers from helping.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: NAACP calls for sports boycott in states where voting rights are threatened
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson about his organization's Out of Bounds campaign that draws a connection between Black student athletes and voting rights.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson about his organization's Out of Bounds campaign that draws a connection between Black student athletes and voting rights.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes. How will this affect deal to end war?
item.description: Do new strikes affect a potential deal to end the war with Iran? NPR's Scott Detrow breaks it down with NPR International Correspondent Aya Batrawy and NPR Pentagon Correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.content:encoded: Do new strikes affect a potential deal to end the war with Iran? NPR's Scott Detrow breaks it down with NPR International Correspondent Aya Batrawy and NPR Pentagon Correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy
